creator: bryan@shatow.net
text:
On 8/7/2012 11:03 AM, Alex Dupre wrote:
> Bryan Drewery ha scritto:
>> However, this does not work in this case as the problem is purely in
>> 'make install' phase. Poudriere does not install RUN_DEPENDS before
>> 'install' as the port should build fine without RUN_DEPENDS being
>> installed (per bapt@).
> 
> I don't know poudriere, but bsd.port.mk checks that RUN_DEPENDS (and
> *not* BUILD_DEPENDS [absurd imho]) are installed for do-install, so the
> behavior is not coherent between the twos.
> 
> ioncube should probably use PHP_VER instead of PHP_VERSION.
> 

poudriere aside, the problem exists here in a fresh jail by just running
'make install'.

PHP_VER is one of 5,53,52, but ioncube wants 5.4,5.3,5.2, etc. Which
means using the same PHP_VERSION!= line in ioncube's Makefile if it is
empty. I see this as a hack.

I think it makes perfect sense that if bsd.php.mk is provding
PHP_VERSION and PHP_SAPI, they should be properly provided in all cases.
Otherwise they are not safe to use in port Makefiles. OTOH, only
devel/ioncube and sysutils/pear-I18Nv2 are currently using PHP_VERSION.


Bryan
